# ANALYSIS: BULL-FILTER10-VOL-SWEEP

**Filed:** 2026-06-16  
**Filer:** chef-nemotron (free-tier autonomous R&D)  
**Type:** parameter_sweep  
**Status:** DRAFT (NEEDS-RATIFICATION per Rule 9)

## Hypothesis

Lowering the vol_mult for the bull buyer pressure filter (filter_10) from the current 0.7 will capture more of the blocked profitable entries (as seen in the near-miss outcome audit showing 2/3 moved favorably avg +0.94 SPY) without adding excessive noise, thereby improving edge_capture on the OP-16 anchor days.

## Mechanism

The bull buyer pressure filter (filter_10) uses a volume multiplier (vol_mult) to determine the threshold for bullish volume sweeps. The sweep tests vol_mult values from 0.3 to 0.9 in 0.1 steps. For each value, we run a 17-month backtest and record n_trades, win rate, P&L, and edge_capture relative to the OP-16 anchor days.

## OP-20 disclosures

1. **Account-size assumption:** qty=28 requires $25K+; $1K paper ~= 14% headline
2. **Sample bias:** 17-month sample (approx 2024-12 to 2026-06) with selection method: all trading days in the period. Overfit risk: parameter sweep without out-of-sample validation.
3. **Out-of-sample:** NEEDS-OOS (no walk-forward held-out window performed in this sweep)
4. **Real-fills:** NEEDS-REAL-FILLS (real-fills check on top 3 J days not done for each vol_mult)
5. **Failure modes:** Worst day: potential for increased losses if vol_mult too low (capturing noise); max drawdown: could increase if false entries add losses; blow-up scenario: if vol_mult too low, engine may take many small losses that accumulate.
6. **Concentration:** If top-5 days = X% of P&L, state X (unknown without backtest)

## Pre-merge gate

Stage-1 backtest for each vol_mult must pass gym validators, followed by walk-forward OOS validation, real-fills check on the top 3 J days (4/29, 5/01, 5/04) for the recommended vol_mult, and confirmation of no regressions on anchor days.

## Confidence

3 / 10 -- No backtest results available; hypothesis based on near-miss audit showing 2/3 blocked entries moved favorably.

## Pre-existing leaderboard impact

This sweep is an analysis of an existing filter (filter_10) and does not conflict with any current leaderboard candidates. It may inform a future filter_change candidate.
